perturbation.original_text: Affiliate is only eligible to earn the Advertising Cost Compensation on sales occurring during the term of this Agreement and fees earned up to the date termination will remain payable only if the related orders are not cancelled or returned.
perturbation.changed_text: Affiliate is eligible to earn the Advertising Cost Compensation on sales occurring during the term of this Agreement. All fees earned up to the date termination will NOT remain payable even if the related orders are not cancelled or returned.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a direct contradiction regarding the payment of advertising cost compensation after termination. The original text states that fees earned up to the termination date remain payable if the orders are not cancelled. The changed text explicitly states that fees earned up to the termination date will not remain payable, even if the orders are valid, creating uncertainty. This affects the expectation of affiliates regarding post-termination earnings, which can affect their overall incentives and the nature of the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: element 5 is authorized to retain final payment for a reasonable time, minimum for 30 days after the end of the respective quarter, to ensure that the correct amount is paid. Affiliate is eligible to earn the Advertising Cost Compensation on sales occurring during the term of this Agreement. All fees earned up to the date termination will NOT remain payable even if the related orders are not cancelled or returned. If the Advertising Cost Compensation was previously paid by element 5, it can be offset or invoiced to Affiliate at element 5's own discretion.
perturbation.justification: The changed text directly contradicts the original text, and thus, the contradicted text, regarding the payment of advertising cost compensation after termination. The original text stated that fees earned up to termination remained payable if orders weren't cancelled, while the changed text explicitly states they will NOT be payable even if orders are valid. The contradicted text also includes the changed text, creating a direct in-text contradiction. This uncertainty directly contradicts the affiliate's expectations regarding earnings and creates a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Affiliate shall integrate the product under the agreement using the appropriate link on its Web site; the link points to the order form operated by element 5. This link is generated by a Link Generator for the respective product under the agreement. The Link Source: SOUTHERN STAR ENERGY INC., SB-2/A, 12/2/2005 Generator is located in the secure area of element 5's Web site in the "Affiliate Control Panel". The link that is generated contains an individualized affiliate user ID by means of which any sales can be attributed to the Affiliate. Proper technical integration of the link is the responsibility of the Affiliate.
perturbation.changed_text: The Affiliate may integrate the product under the agreement using the link on its Web site; the link points to a general information page. The link is generated for products under the agreement. The link is located outside the secure area of element 5's Web site in the "Public Information Section". The link that is generated does not contain an individualized affiliate user ID meaning any sales can't be accurately attributed to the Affiliate. element 5 holds responsibility for the technical integration of the link.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates the Affiliate to use a specific link generated by the link generator in the affiliate control panel and the link containing a personalized affiliate user ID to track sales. By contrast, the modified text states that the affiliate can use the link provided on the website which directs to a public information section without a personalized user ID. This means sales cannot be accurately attributed to the affiliate. This creates a contradiction as the affiliate is not responsible for technical integration and sales may not be correctly attributed, conflicting with the affiliate's compensation as a result of this change.
perturbation.contradicted_text: element 5 will pay Affiliate its advertising costs (including Vat, if any, on presentation of a valid VAT invoice) if an end user arrives via the link integrated in Affiliate's Web site at the order page hosted by element 5 for the respective Software Source: SOUTHERN STAR ENERGY INC., SB-2/A, 12/2/2005 Publisher's Product under this Agreement and end user uses the automatic ordering system that carries out the registration and payment processing, so that the Affiliate's link is deemed to be directly causative for the entry of the End User into the agreement with the Software Publisher and End User's payment, proving that all requirements are irrevocably met ("agreements arising in a qualified manner"). Affiliate is only eligible to earn the Advertising Cost Compensation on sales occurring during the term of this Agreement and fees earned up to the date termination will remain payable only if the related orders are not cancelled or returned.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the Affiliate will receive advertising costs if an end user arrives via the link integrated in the Affiliate's Web site at the order page, and the Affiliate's link is deemed directly causative for the entry of the End User. The changed text, however, states that the link that is generated does not contain an individualized affiliate user ID, meaning any sales can't be accurately attributed to the Affiliate, conflicting with the contradicted text. Since sales cannot be accurately attributed to the affiliate, it directly breaks the condition that 'the Affiliate's link is deemed to be directly causative for the entry of the End User' in order for the Affiliate to get paid advertising costs. This creates a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Affiliate shall take due care to ensure that no unauthorized parties learn the password.
perturbation.changed_text: The Affiliate shall share the user ID and password with its staff to ensure proper management of the account.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause mandates strict confidentiality of the password, forbidding the sharing of access details with unauthorized parties. The modified text instructs the Affiliate to share the password with its staff, thereby explicitly contradicting the confidentiality requirement. This creates uncertainty about the security and responsibility associated with account access. The contradiction introduces risks regarding unauthorized access and potential misuse of the Affiliate's account, and potential disputes over liability for actions taken under the account.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (1) After successfully completing the registration process for participation in the Program, the Affiliate will be sent a user ID and a password to access to the "Affiliate Control Panel". With the user ID and password, the "Affiliate Control Panel" provides the Affiliate access, via a secure online connection, to a secure area on element 5's Web server so that the Affiliate may, at any time, view the number of software sales the Affiliate has referred, as well as change other settings. (2) When using the user ID and password, the Affiliate shall observe the following obligations and precautions: (i) Only the Affiliate may use the user ID and password. (ii) The Affiliate shall share the user ID and password with its staff to ensure proper management of the account. (iii) If the Affiliate loses its password or if there is a possibility that an unauthorized party has learned the user ID and password, the Affiliate shall immediately report this to element 5, which can then block access by the Affiliate to the Control Panel. All actions carried out with the user ID and password will be attributed to the Affiliate.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the Affiliate should ensure no unauthorized parties learn the password. The changed text instructs the Affiliate to share the password with its staff, which directly contradicts the confidentiality requirement implied in the original clause. This contradicts section (2)(i) of the contradicted text, which states 'Only the Affiliate may use the user ID and password.' and creates an inconsistency, making it unclear who is authorized to use the password, causing uncertainty regarding security protocols and potential liability, therefore, a strong contradiction exists.
